source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/04_제품별 인허가 진행 문서/02_ADD (HAD1417MCW)/▶ FDA/기술문서/Usability Engineering/09-02. Usability Validation Plan (Doc.No. A-TCF-5-039(rev.0)).docx

document.title: Usability Validation Plan for ADD Abyz Digital Detector

sourceSummary.oneLine: Usability validation plan for ADD Abyz Digital Detector defining usability test participants, scenarios, metrics, goals, reporting, and referenced standards.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Used to plan usability testing during development of ADD and evaluate usability metrics against pre-approved goals.
keyPoints: The document describes a test plan for conducting a usability test during development of ADD.
A total of 7 participants should meet the stated user profile for the usability test.
Usability metrics include scenario completion success rates, adherence to dialog scripts, error rates, subjective evaluations, and time-to-completion.
Primary operating function scenarios include power, cable, LAN, communication, detector power-on, patient arrangement, and detector calibration tasks.
Usability goals are 100% completion rate for each usability task and below 4 total errors for each usability task.

concepts.name: Usability validation
concepts.evidence: This document describes a test plan for conducting a usability test during the development of ADD.

concepts.name: Usability metrics
concepts.evidence: Usability metrics refers to user performance measured against specific performance goals necessary to satisfy usability requirements.

concepts.name: Critical errors
concepts.evidence: Critical errors are unresolved errors during the process of completing the task or errors that produce an incorrect outcome regarding the safety.

concepts.name: Non-critical errors
concepts.evidence: Non-critical errors are an error that would not have an impact on the final output of the task but would result in the task being completed less efficiently.

concepts.name: Subjective evaluations
concepts.evidence: Subjective opinions about specific tasks, time to perform each task, features, and functionality will be surveyed and collected via questionnaires.

concepts.name: Use scenarios
concepts.evidence: The usability tasks were derived from test scenarios developed from use cases and/or with the assistance of a subject-matter expert.

concepts.name: Risk management for medical devices
concepts.evidence: EN ISO 14971:2007 Medical devices - Application of risk management to medical devices

concepts.name: Usability engineering for medical devices
concepts.evidence: EN 62366:2008 Medical devices - Application of usability engineering to medical devices

evidence.claim: The document is titled Usability Validation Plan for ADD Abyz Digital Detector.
evidence.evidenceText: Usability Validation Plan for ADD Abyz Digital Detector

evidence.claim: The report number is A-TCF-5-039 with revision Rev.0.

evidence.evidenceText: Prepared R&D team Jung, Woosuk APR/18/2020 Review R&D Manager Lee, Dong jin APR/18/2020 Approval Quality Manager Hur Namkyu APR/18/2020
Rev. No. 0 Rev. date APR/18/2020 Revision contents Establishment

evidence.claim: The plan is for conducting a usability test during development of ADD.
evidence.evidenceText: This document describes a test plan for conducting a usability test during the development of ADD.

evidence.claim: The usability test objectives include constructing user attribute benchmarks, identifying design problems, improving product design and production efficiency, and maximizing end-user satisfaction.
evidence.evidenceText: The usability test objectives are: To construct the benchmarks of user attributes To identify potential design problems to be solve To improve product design and production efficiency To complete end-user satisfaction maximization

evidence.claim: The usability test calls for a total of 7 participants meeting the stated user profile.
evidence.evidenceText: A total 7 participants should be at least equal to the following User Profile used in the Usability test.

evidence.claim: Participants receive an overview of the usability test procedure, equipment, and software.
evidence.evidenceText: The participants will receive and overview of the usability test procedure, equipment and software.

evidence.claim: ADD is used in a typical operating environment described in the usability engineering report.
evidence.evidenceText: The ADD will be used in a typical operating environment described in usability engineering report.

evidence.claim: Usability metrics include scenario completion success rates, adherence to dialog scripts, error rates, subjective evaluations, and time-to-completion.
evidence.evidenceText: Scenario completion success rates, adherence to dialog scripts, error rates, and subjective evaluations will be used. Time-to-completion of scenarios will also be collected.

evidence.claim: The post-test satisfaction questionnaire uses a 5-point rating scale from 1 Strongly disagree to 5 Strongly agree.
evidence.evidenceText: The 5-point rating scale ranged from 1 (Strongly disagree) to 5 (Strongly agree)

evidence.claim: Primary operating function scenarios include user manual familiarization, cable connections, powering on the detector, patient positioning, and detector calibration by PC software.
evidence.evidenceText: Use scenarios related to the primary operating functions. Familiarize yourself with the user manual... Connecting the power cord... Power on the detector... Arrange the patient... Calibration of detector by S/W in PC

evidence.claim: Reasonably foreseeable worst case scenarios include wiring disconnection, not attending to detector readiness before X-ray exposure, wrong interface cable wiring, dropping, excessive force, wrong calibration, high temperature, water or humidity, and patient positioning by holding a detector.
evidence.evidenceText: Use scenarios related to the reasonably foreseeable worst case... Wiring disconnection to Detector... Not attention to Detector’s ready before X-ray exposures... Wrong wiring... Dropping Applying excessive force Wrong calibration... High temperature... Water/humidity... Positioning by holding a Detector

evidence.claim: The usability goal for completion rate is 100% for each usability task.
evidence.evidenceText: Usability Goal: A total completion rate of 100 % is the goal for each usability task.

evidence.claim: The usability goal for errors is below 4 total errors for each usability task.
evidence.evidenceText: Usability Goal: A total errors of below 4 is the goal for each usability task.

evidence.claim: A usability verification and evaluation report will be provided at the conclusion of the usability test.
evidence.evidenceText: The Usability verification & evaluation report will be provided at the conclusion of the usability test.

evidence.claim: The document references EN ISO 14971:2007, EN 60601-1-6:2010 + A1:2015, and EN 62366:2008.

reviewFlags.reason: Authority or jurisdiction is not stated in the document text; source metadata signals MFDS/domestic while the source path includes FDA.
